bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s only offer was all valueF, which is very low utility for me and does not yet show any meaningful concession pattern.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-max utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my previous all-best offer. The opponent has twice offered all valueF, which is very low utility for me and shows no real concession yet, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight and high-value issues while conceding only issueC from valueA to valueB.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has only offered all valueF and then a tiny move to issueA:valueE, which is still very low utility for me, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and other top values while varying my prior bid with a modest concession on issueC.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.836 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has barely conceded, moving only issueA from valueF to valueE while keeping the rest at valueF. I will continue holding firm on my most important issueA and make only a small variation on issueC to avoid repeating my prior bid exactly.

At t=0.08 this is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my last offer. The opponent has held near valueF on most issues and only moved issueA from valueF to valueE, so their bids still have very low utility for me and do not yet justify major concessions.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent has mostly held firm around very low-value offers for me, with only a slight move from issueA:valueF to valueE, so I will keep my most important issues strong and concede only modestly on issueB.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on issueC, where the opponent’s latest offer moved from valueF to valueC. The opponent has conceded only slightly overall and is still offering low value to me, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and keep strong values on issueB and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.80 since it is still early at t=0.14, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent has mostly held firm on valueF for issueB/issueC/issueD while slowly moving on issueA, so I will hold my most important issueA at valueA and make a modest concession on issueB instead of repeating my last bid.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession to keep movement going. The opponent’s offers remain very low for me and mostly stuck on valueF, with only small movement on issueA and one signal around issueC:valueC, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and concede mainly on issueC/B where the cost is lower.

At t=0.18 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility offer while making a modest concession to keep movement visible. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueB/issueC/issueD toward valueF and has only conceded issueA from valueF/E to valueD, but their offers remain very low for me, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issueA and give ground mainly on lower-cost issueC/issueB.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.20, so I should not make large concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueB and issueD at valueF, but their latest offer moved issueC to valueD, which is a relatively low-cost concession for me compared with giving up issueA or issueD. I will therefore match issueC: valueD while holding my strongest positions elsewhere.

At t=0.22 I am still targeting a fairly high utility bid, but I can make a meaningful concession on lower-weight issues to improve acceptability. The opponent has moved from all valueF to issueA:valueD and issueC:valueD, while holding issueB and issueD at valueF, so I will keep my most important issueA and issueB strong but concede issueC to valueD and issueD to valueF; this gives me about 0.712 utility and avoids repeating my last bid.

At t=0.24 I am still targeting a reasonably high utility offer, but I will make a meaningful concession compared with my strongest bids. The opponent has repeatedly held issueB=valueF and issueD=valueF, while moving somewhat on issueA and issueC, so I concede issueD and keep strong positions on my highest-weight issueA and a decent value on issueB.

I am targeting a still-solid utility around 0.62, since time is only at 0.26 and there is no need to concede to the opponent’s low-utility proposal yet. The opponent has moved from all valueF to issueA:valueD and issueC:valueD, but is holding firm on issueB:valueF and issueD:valueF, so I keep my most important issueA at valueA while conceding further on lower-weight issueB and issueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility bid around 0.57, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has been slowly improving their offers from all valueF toward issueA/valueD and issueC/valueD. Their bids consistently keep issueB and issueD at valueF, so I am conceding those contested lower-weight issues while holding firm on my highest-weight issueA at valueA and accepting their signaled issueC/valueD.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.51: still well above reservation, but with a meaningful concession now that we are at t=0.30. The opponent has repeatedly held issueB and issueD at valueF and has moved issueC to valueD, so I match those signals while conceding issueA only to valueB rather than all the way to their valueD. This avoids simply repeating my last bid and tests whether a smaller issueA concession can unlock agreement.

At t=0.32 I am targeting a moderate-utility compromise rather than dropping all the way to the opponent's repeated bid, since there is still substantial time left. The opponent has held firm on issueB=valueF, issueC=valueD, and issueD=valueF, while only conceding on issueA up to valueD; I will keep a stronger position on high-weight issueA but align with their apparent priorities on the other issues.

I targeted a still-positive utility around 0.41 while making a meaningful concession because the opponent has held firm on issueB=valueF, issueC=valueD, and issueD=valueF for many rounds. At t=0.34 there is not deadline panic, but my last exact bid was repeated and rejected, so I am conceding mainly on issueA while preserving validity and matching the opponent’s most consistent recent signals.
